## Kit Preamble — qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator

If exit code is 1 (update available), show the output to the user once. Do not block the workflow.

If the result is `true`:

3. Maximum 3 reports per session. Skip if a report for the same step already exists.
4. Do NOT stop the workflow to file reports — do it inline.
5. Only report kit/skill issues (unclear instructions, missing checkpoints, bad ergonomics). Do NOT report user-app bugs or network errors.

## What this skill does

Compares two NAND fab strategies for meeting a QLC:TLC market demand mix.
Both strategies sweep the SAME QLC:TLC ratio `r` (0-100%, adjustable via
slider), but each line brings its OWN independent 100%-utilization max
wafer capacity — dedicated and combo (and even QLC-recipe vs TLC-recipe
within combo) are separate investments/lines, so their max capacities are
NOT a single shared pool that gets divided up:

1. **분리 생산 (dedicated)** — a QLC-only line AND a separate TLC-only line
   (own die design each, own investment, own max capa). At ratio `r` the
   QLC line runs at `r%` of its OWN `qlc_max_capa`, and the TLC line runs
   at `(100-r)%` of its OWN `tlc_max_capa` — the two max capacities can
   differ since they're different lines.
2. **콤보 생산 (combo)** — one flexible, physically single line built around
   the QLC die design that can also run a TLC recipe, ALWAYS 100% utilized
   (time-sharing `r%` QLC-recipe / `(100-r)%` TLC-recipe, never idle).
   `combo_capex_per_wafer` / `combo_gross_die` are shared by both recipes
   since it's one physical line and die — only density/yield AND each
   recipe's own max capa (`combo_qlc_max_capa` / `combo_tlc_max_capa`,
   usually close but not required to match) are recipe-specific.

Cost and bit production come straight from physical parameters — the only
tuning coefficients are the two flat, shared dev-cost conversion rates
below (headcount and mask cost):

```
bit_per_wafer = density_per_die * grossdie_per_wafer * (mature_yield_pct / 100)
cost(line)    = (capex_per_10k_wafer / 10000) * wafer_count
```

`*_mature_yield` fields are entered as a percentage (`82` means 82%) — this
is the FULLY RAMPED yield the line/recipe converges toward once mature, not
its yield at any particular point in time (see "Yield ramp-up" below).
`*_capex_per_wafer` fields are entered as cost per 10,000 wafers
(`capex_per_10k_wafer / 10000` gives the effective per-wafer rate used above
and in dev cost below); `*_density` fields are labeled Gb/Wafer and feed
`bit_per_wafer` unchanged — the resulting bit totals are Gb-denominated,
converted to GB (÷8) only at the display/output layer (see "Bit/GB display
conversion" below).

**Yield ramp-up (Normalized Exponential Saturation)**: each of the four
lines/recipes (`qlc`, `tlc`, `combo_qlc`, `combo_tlc`) carries a pair of
fields instead of a single yield number — `*_mature_yield` (% once fully
ramped) and `*_yield_ramp_coef` (1/month; larger = faster ramp-up):

```
yield_fraction(t) = mature_yield_fraction * (1 - exp(-ramp_coef * t))
```

`t` is months elapsed since THAT line/recipe's OWN production start (t=0 at
start of production) — combo's QLC recipe, combo's TLC recipe, dedicated's
QLC line, and dedicated's TLC line each ramp independently from their own
t=0. The original "monthly" snapshot table/charts and the crossover
comparison are UNCHANGED by this — they still assume a fully ramped line
(`mature_yield_fraction` used directly, exactly like the old `*_yield`
field). Only the 5-year cumulative bit total (see below) actually walks
this ramp curve month by month. Two dedicated reference charts
(`calc.yield_ramp_curve`, mirrored by `render_html.py`'s
`combinedRampPoints()`/`yieldRampFraction()`) plot this curve from t=0 to
t=36 months, purely so `*_mature_yield`/`*_yield_ramp_coef` can be tuned by
eye — see "Six charts" below.

On top of per-wafer cost, each scenario carries a one-time development cost
with TWO components, both added once to every row's total cost regardless
of ratio: **headcount required** (`combo_headcount` / `dedicated_headcount`)
and **mask sets consumed** (`combo_mask_count` / `dedicated_mask_count`),
converted via two shared (not combo/dedicated-split) coefficients,
`coef_cost_per_headcount` and `coef_cost_per_mask`:

```
dev_cost(combo)      = combo_headcount * coef_cost_per_headcount
                      + combo_mask_count * coef_cost_per_mask
dev_cost(dedicated)  = dedicated_headcount * coef_cost_per_headcount
                      + dedicated_mask_count * coef_cost_per_mask
```

There is no ER-wafer cost term here — `wafer_total * capex_rate_per_wafer`
(the regular per-wafer cost, unchanged) already pays for every wafer
processed, ER or not, so a separate one-time "ER wafer count × capex" line
would double-count. ER wafers instead feed bit PRODUCTION, not cost — see
"ER period (t95) and the sale/ER wafer split" below.

**ER period (t95) and the sale/ER wafer split**: each of the four
lines/recipes (`qlc`, `tlc`, `combo_qlc`, `combo_tlc`) reaches 95% of ITS
OWN mature yield at a month count that depends ONLY on its own
`*_yield_ramp_coef` (the mature-yield value itself cancels out):

```
t95 = ln(20) / yield_ramp_coef
```

`t=0..t95` (months since that line's/recipe's own production start) is that
line's/recipe's own "ER(엔지니어링 런)/qualification period" —
`calc.yield_ramp_t95()`, mirrored by `render_html.py`'s `yieldRampT95()`.
`er_wafer_combo` / `er_wafer_dedicated` are each a **monthly ER-wafer
consumption RATE** (wafer/month), not a one-time total. Crucially, `wafer_total`
(the line's normal ratio-swept wafer volume — `qlc_max_capa * r/100` etc.,
unchanged) never grows because of ER wafers: ER wafers are carved OUT of
it, not produced on top of it. In every month inside a line's/recipe's own
ER period, up to `min(er_wafer_rate, wafer_total)` of that month's wafers
are consumed as ER, and the remainder is sold; outside the ER period, 100%
is sold:

```
sale_wafer(t) = wafer_total - (min(er_wafer_rate, wafer_total) if t <= t95 else 0)
```

`er_wafer_combo` applies, independently and in full (NOT split 50/50), to
BOTH combo's QLC recipe and combo's TLC recipe — each has its own t95 and
its own `wafer_total`, but draws on the same monthly rate. `er_wafer_dedicated`
applies the same way to both the dedicated QLC line and the dedicated TLC
line — this mirrors how `combo_headcount`/`dedicated_headcount` are also
single scenario-level values used as-is on both lines/recipes, not split
between them (`calc.ER_WAFER_RATE_ASSUMPTION` states this explicitly;
restate it in `report.md`).

The TOTAL bit production formula is entirely UNCHANGED —
`wafer_total * density * gross_die * yield_fraction(t)` already implicitly
includes whatever ER wafers were processed that month (they're good die
too, just not sold). What's new is a separate SALE-only figure:

```
sale_bit(t) = sale_wafer(t) * density * gross_die * yield_fraction(t)
```

Both figures are summed month-by-month over each cumulative window (see
"Cumulative bit production windows" below) — `total_bit_total` (unchanged
value) and a NEW `sale_bit_total` per window, per scenario
(`calc.line_ramp_stats()` + the `window_totals()` closure inside
`calc.sweep()`, mirrored by `render_html.py`'s `lineRampStats()`/
`windowTotals()`).

**Cost calculation modes (`cost_mode`, optional, default `model`)**: bit
production is ALWAYS computed from the physical model
(density/gross_die/yield-ramp/max_capa/ER split), regardless of this
setting — it affects ONLY cost.
- `model` (default): the capex + dev-cost model above (unchanged behavior).
- `direct`: bypass the entire capex/dev-cost model. Two fields,
  `combo_direct_cost_per_gb` / `dedicated_direct_cost_per_gb`, entered
  DIRECTLY in **cent/GB** (ratio-independent flat numbers), are used as-is
  for the Cost/GB charts (both monthly and cumulative) instead of the
  swept, model-derived cost/GB. `visualization.html` shows a "원가 계산 모드"
  radio toggle; picking "GB당 원가 직접 입력" disables (greys out) every
  capex/dev-cost input field (`calc.COST_MODEL_ONLY_KEYS`) and those fields
  are skipped by required-field validation — they don't block computation.
  `er_wafer_combo` / `er_wafer_dedicated` are deliberately NOT in that list
  — they feed the bit-production sale/ER split, which matters in BOTH cost
  modes, so those two fields stay enabled and required regardless of
  `cost_mode`. The results panel's Total-cost/Cost-per-bit table cells and
  the one-time dev-cost / full-buildout text lines show "—" (not
  applicable) in this mode rather than a stale, model-based number computed
  from ignored inputs.

**Bit/GB display conversion**: 1 GB = 8 Gb. The raw sweep math (bit totals,
cost/bit, crossover) stays entirely in Gb — nothing above changes. Only
`visualization.html`'s charts and `report.md`'s GB-denominated figures apply
the ÷8 conversion, via `calc.bit_total_gb` / each row's `*_bit_total_gb` /
`*_cost_per_gb` fields (mirrored in `render_html.py`'s JS). The
bit-production chart's y-axis is fixed at **억GB** (10^8 GB); the cost
chart's y-axis is fixed at **cent/GB** — `{currency_unit}/GB` numbers round
to ~0 on a linear axis once `currency_unit` is 억원-scale (the axis used to
look empty), so the Cost chart converts the already-억원-denominated
cost/GB figure into cent/GB via `exchange_rate_krw_per_usd` (원/달러) when
`cost_mode == "model"` (`calc.cost_per_gb_eokwon_to_cent`, mirrored by
`render_html.py`'s `costPerGbEokwonToCent()`) — when `cost_mode ==
"direct"`, the two direct cent/GB inputs are used as-is instead, with no
eokwon conversion. This conversion applies **only** to the Cost chart's
axis — `report.md` and the results table's Cost/Cost-per-bit rows are
unaffected (and show "—" entirely in direct mode, see above). Both chart
axes are drawn directly on the y-axis itself — a rotated unit label plus
numeric tick values, not just the chart title — so neither axis ever reads
as an arbitrary, unitless scale.

**Cumulative bit production windows (1-year AND 5-year, no development-months
skip)**: production is assumed to start at month 1 of BOTH windows — there is
no more shared "development/ramp months" parameter (`dev_ramp_months` is
GONE entirely; each line's/recipe's own t95, above, replaces it). The
5-year (60-month) window is unchanged in spirit from before; a NEW 1-year
(12-month) window is computed the exact same way, to sit alongside it:

```
N_year_bit_total(line)      = wafer_count(line) * sum_{t=1}^{N_MONTHS} density*gross_die*yield_fraction(t; line)
N_year_sale_bit_total(line) = N_year_bit_total(line) - (ER wafers' own bit contribution during the ER period)
N_year_cost_total(line)     = monthly_wafer_cost(line) * N_MONTHS + dev_cost(line)   # dev_cost added ONCE
```

(`N_MONTHS` is 12 or 60; `calc.cumulative_ramp_bit_per_wafer()` /
`calc.line_ramp_stats()`, mirrored by `render_html.py`'s
`cumulativeRampBitPerWafer()`/`lineRampStats()`). Cost does NOT depend on
yield (paid per wafer processed, not per good bit), so its formula is
unchanged from before other than dropping the development-months
deferral. Same cent/GB display conversion as the monthly Cost chart (model
mode) or the direct cent/GB inputs as-is (direct mode).

**Six charts** — the original two (monthly bit production, monthly
cost/GB, formulas unchanged except the yield rename) plus four more:

1. **Bit 생산량 vs QLC 비율 (억GB)** and **2. Cost/GB vs QLC 비율 (cent/GB)**
   — monthly snapshot, mature yield by default (see "기준 시점 toggle"
   below for the one exception to that default).
2. **5년 누적 Bit 생산량 vs QLC 비율 (억GB)**: the 5-year TOTAL bit figure
   from "Cumulative bit production windows" above, plotted per ratio.
3. **5년 누적 Cost/GB vs QLC 비율 (cent/GB)**: the 5-year cost figure from
   above.
4. **QLC 수율 램프업 (%, x축 = 생산 시작 후 경과 개월 0~36)**: dedicated's
   QLC line vs combo's QLC recipe, both drawn from the Normalized
   Exponential Saturation formula using `qlc_mature_yield`/
   `qlc_yield_ramp_coef` and `combo_qlc_mature_yield`/
   `combo_qlc_yield_ramp_coef` respectively. FIXED reference curve — does
   NOT move with the QLC:TLC ratio slider (no dashed ratio indicator drawn
   on it) — purely so `*_mature_yield`/`*_yield_ramp_coef` can be tuned by
   eye (`calc.yield_ramp_curve`, mirrored by `render_html.py`'s
   `combinedRampPoints()`).
5. **TLC 수율 램프업 (%, x축 = 생산 시작 후 경과 개월 0~36)**: same
   treatment for dedicated's TLC line vs combo's TLC recipe.

**"기준 시점" (yield basis) toggle — charts 1-2 AND the results table's
combo/dedicated "Bit 생산량"/"Cost / bit" cells ONLY, nothing else**: a radio
choice, right under the QLC:TLC ratio slider, between "성숙 수율 기준"
(default — behaves exactly as before, mature_yield used as-is) and "특정
개월 기준" (activates a 1-60 month slider, styled/operated exactly like the
QLC:TLC ratio slider). Picking a month `t` recomputes each of the four
lines'/recipes' (`qlc`/`tlc`/`combo_qlc`/`combo_tlc`) bit-per-wafer using
THAT line's/recipe's own actual `yield_fraction(t) = mature_yield × (1 -
exp(-ramp_coef × t))` at month `t` instead of the mature yield, and recomputes
Bit 생산량/Cost per bit/Cost per GB from that — Total cost is untouched
(cost never depends on yield, only on wafer count processed). The page also
shows, as reference text, which month is selected and each line's/recipe's
own ramp progress at that month (% of ITS mature yield reached, independent
of the mature yield value — same `ln(20)`-style cancellation as t95 above).
This is implemented as a SEPARATE "display sweep"
(`render_html.py`'s `computeDisplaySweep`, reusing `calc.yield_ramp_fraction`'s
formula and mirrored by `calc.yield_ramp_progress_pct` for the reference-text
percentage) layered on top of the base sweep — it is a pure client-side
display lens, not persisted to `sweep.csv`/`sweep.json`/`report.md`, and it
never reaches the 5-year/1-year cumulative rows, the two yield-ramp reference
charts, the live verdict text, or the crossover comparison, all of which
keep reading the base sweep and stay mature-yield-fixed regardless of this
toggle. Reloading the page (or a fresh run) always starts back on "성숙 수율
기준".

The 1-year cumulative TOTAL bit figures (both scenarios) are NOT a separate
chart — they're shown as live numbers instead: in the results table (next to
the 5-year row) and, per the "Dynamic intro stats" requirement, in the
top-of-page intro paragraph (`#introStats`), which updates on every slider
move to show 콤보/분리 생산's current-ratio 1-year AND 5-year TOTAL bit
production side by side.

**전체 투자비용 (Max capa 풀 빌드아웃 기준) is TEXT, not a chart**: two
ratio-INDEPENDENT numbers — what each scenario would cost if it built every
one of its lines out to its own 100%-utilization max capacity, regardless
of the ratio actually served (`calc.full_buildout()`, mirrored by
`render_html.py`'s JS `fullBuildout()`) — are shown as a single line of text
in the left info panel (`전체 투자비용 (Max capa 풀 빌드아웃 기준): 콤보 XX /
분리 YY`), not a chart, since they're two flat scalars rather than
something a line chart adds value to. Dedicated sums its two independent
lines: `qlc_capex_rate * qlc_max_capa + tlc_capex_rate * tlc_max_capa +
dedicated_dev_cost`. Combo is physically ONE line but carries two max-capa
fields (`combo_qlc_max_capa` / `combo_tlc_max_capa`); this skill uses
**`combo_qlc_max_capa` as the representative buildout capacity**
(`combo_capex_rate * combo_qlc_max_capa + combo_dev_cost`) — see
`calc.COMBO_FULL_CAPEX_ASSUMPTION` for the stated rationale. This is a
stated modeling choice, not a supplied number — restate it in
`report.md`/README whenever these figures are reported. It is only
meaningful when `cost_mode == "model"`; in `direct` mode the text line
states plainly that it doesn't apply (the capex model is bypassed).

**Every one of the 30 required fab parameters is also a live input field in
`visualization.html`**. Real fab numbers (capex, density, gross die, yield
ramp, capacity, ER wafers, headcount, mask count) are often not allowed to
leave the fab in a file, so `visualization.html` is a complete standalone
calculator: type values directly into the browser and the sweep/charts/
verdict recompute live. A params file (or the samples) is still useful to
pre-fill the page, but it is never required to use the calculator.

**`visualization.html`'s initial values are hand-editable in a plain text
editor, without FrameAI or Python.** The seed data is embedded as a
pretty-printed, indented `var INITIAL_PAYLOAD = {...};` JS literal (built by
`render_html._payload_js_literal`), not a compact one-line JSON blob — every
key inside `INITIAL_PAYLOAD.params` (capex, density, yield ramp, max capa,
headcount/mask, the two dev-cost coefficients, `cost_mode`/direct-cost
inputs, plus `currency_unit`/`bit_unit`/`ratio_step`) carries an inline
`// ...` comment stating its meaning and unit. An HTML comment directly
above the script block explains, in Korean, that editing those numbers and
saving, then reopening the file in a browser, makes the edited values the
new initial values — this is the intended way to hand a single `.html` file
to someone else and let them adjust real fab numbers with only Notepad. The
`sweep`/`crossover`/`dev_cost`/`full_buildout`/`best_*` parts of the payload
are last-run reference data only — the page always recomputes everything
from `params` client-side, so editing those other sections has no effect.

**Default is sample-filled, not blank.** When no params file or `key=value`
text is available (CLI run with no `--in`, or an empty chat invocation),
this skill no longer opens an empty calculator — it pre-fills
`visualization.html` with the bundled `samples/sample-1.txt` synthetic
values (clearly marked with an on-page banner) so the user sees a working
example — a real chart, a real crossover — the moment it opens, instead of
a wall of empty fields. A genuinely blank screen (every field empty, e.g.
for typing in real fab numbers that can't leave the building) is still
available on request: say so in chat ("blank로 줘" / "빈 화면으로"), or the
in-page **"전체 비우기"** button, or `render_html.py --blank` directly.

This lets the calculator answer the real strategic question: at high TLC
demand, dedicated's true TLC recipe is usually more bit-efficient than
forcing a QLC die into TLC mode; at low TLC demand, combo's cheaper shared
capex (one investment instead of two) usually wins. The `crossover` field
in the output marks the QLC:TLC ratio where the better strategy switches
(based on the monthly, mature-yield snapshot).

Outputs, for every run:

1. `sweep.csv` / `sweep.json` — combo AND dedicated scenarios, both swept
   across the same shared-capacity mix ratio (bit production — total AND
   sale-only for the 1-year/5-year cumulative fields — total cost, cost/bit
   at each ratio for each scenario, plus `full_buildout`/`yield_ramp_curves`/
   `t95_months`/`er_wafer_rate_assumption`/`cost_mode`/`direct_cost_per_gb`
   top-level fields).
2. `visualization.html` — self-contained interactive HTML: a 0-100 slider
   for the QLC ratio, live-updating numbers (including a dynamic intro
   paragraph showing each scenario's current-ratio 1-year/5-year TOTAL bit
   production), and SIX inline-SVG line charts (monthly bit production,
   monthly cost/GB, 5-year cumulative bit production, 5-year cumulative
   cost/GB, QLC 수율 램프업, TLC 수율 램프업) with combo and dedicated both
   drawn as solid lines side by side, plus text-only full max-capa buildout
   and ER-period figures. A "원가 계산 모드" radio toggle picks model-based
   vs direct GB-cost calculation — `er_wafer_combo`/`er_wafer_dedicated`
   stay enabled in both modes since they feed bit production. No external
   JS/CSS dependency — opens standalone.
3. `report.md` — plain-language summary: best ratio for combo and for
   dedicated on bit production and on cost/bit, the crossover ratio (if
   any) where combo starts beating dedicated on cost/bit, the one-time dev
   costs (headcount + mask only) and the ER-wafer-rate assumption (model
   mode) or the direct GB-cost inputs (direct mode), the 1-year/5-year
   cumulative TOTAL and SALE-only bit totals, each line's/recipe's own t95,
   and a recommendation. Language matches the input (Korean params/text →
   Korean report; English → English).

## Input format

A params file is **optional** — it only pre-fills `visualization.html`;
every field can also be typed directly into the browser (see "What this
skill does" above). When a params file is used, it is a `key=value` text
file (`#` starts a comment, blank lines ignored). All 30 required keys —
see `automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/samples/sample-1.txt` for a
fully worked example:

```
# Dedicated (fully independent per line — own die design, own max capa each)
# capex_per_wafer = cost per 10,000 wafers; density = Gb/Wafer;
# mature_yield = % (fully ramped); yield_ramp_coef = ramp speed, 1/month
qlc_capex_per_wafer / qlc_density / qlc_gross_die / qlc_mature_yield / qlc_yield_ramp_coef / qlc_max_capa
tlc_capex_per_wafer / tlc_density / tlc_gross_die / tlc_mature_yield / tlc_yield_ramp_coef / tlc_max_capa

# Combo (one physical line/die — capex_per_wafer/gross_die shared across both
# recipes; each recipe still gets its own mature_yield/yield_ramp_coef/max capa)
combo_capex_per_wafer / combo_gross_die
combo_qlc_density / combo_qlc_mature_yield / combo_qlc_yield_ramp_coef / combo_qlc_max_capa
combo_tlc_density / combo_tlc_mature_yield / combo_tlc_yield_ramp_coef / combo_tlc_max_capa

# Monthly ER-wafer consumption RATE (wafer/월, NOT a one-time total) —
# applied independently, in full, to BOTH lines/recipes of its scenario
# during each one's OWN ER period (t <= that line's/recipe's own t95 =
# ln(20)/yield_ramp_coef). Feeds bit production (sale/ER split); NOT
# ignored by cost_mode=direct (unlike everything else in this block).
er_wafer_combo / er_wafer_dedicated

# One-time dev cost, per scenario — headcount and mask sets (converted via
# the two shared coefficients below). Ignored entirely when cost_mode=direct.
combo_headcount / dedicated_headcount
combo_mask_count / dedicated_mask_count

# Shared dev-cost conversion coefficients (one pair, not split combo/dedicated)
coef_cost_per_headcount / coef_cost_per_mask
```

Optional display/sweep settings: `currency_unit`, `bit_unit` (display labels
only, e.g. `억원`/`$M`, `Gb`/`TB`), `ratio_step` (slider granularity in
percent, default `5`), `exchange_rate_krw_per_usd` (원/달러, default `1300`
— feeds ONLY the Cost chart's fixed cent/GB axis conversion when
`cost_mode == "model"`; does not affect `report.md`, the results table, or
any other output), `cost_mode` (`model` or `direct`, default `model` — see
"Cost calculation modes" above), `combo_direct_cost_per_gb` /
`dedicated_direct_cost_per_gb` (cent/GB, default `0` — only consumed when
`cost_mode == direct`). There is no `dev_ramp_months` anymore — removed
outright; see "ER period (t95) and the sale/ER wafer split" above.

**Units are free-form as long as every field stays internally consistent,
EXCEPT the conventions below, which the calculator itself converts**
(everything else — density's absolute scale, gross die, headcount/mask
counts — the calculator does not convert):
- `*_mature_yield`: percentage, e.g. `82` means 82% once fully ramped
  (divided by 100 internally). This is NOT the yield at any specific point
  in time — see `*_yield_ramp_coef` below.
- `*_yield_ramp_coef`: ramp-up speed, 1/month (larger = faster ramp to
  `*_mature_yield`). `yield_fraction(t) = mature_yield/100 * (1 -
  exp(-ramp_coef * t))`, `t` = months since THAT line/recipe's own
  production start.
- `*_capex_per_wafer`: cost per **10,000 wafers**, e.g. `6200` means 6200
  currency-units to produce 10,000 wafers (divided by 10,000 internally to
  get the per-wafer rate). Ignored when `cost_mode == direct`.
- `*_density`: labeled Gb/Wafer; the raw `density * grossdie_per_wafer *
  mature_yield` formula is unchanged — only the resulting bit totals get an
  additional ÷8 Gb→GB conversion at the display/output layer (charts,
  report.md GB figures), never in the underlying sweep math.
- `*_max_capa`: wafer/월 (wafer count per month), calculation unchanged.
- `er_wafer_combo` / `er_wafer_dedicated`: MONTHLY ER-wafer consumption RATE
  (wafer/월, NOT a one-time total) — carved out of that line's/recipe's own
  `wafer_total` during its own ER period (`t <= t95`), not produced on top
  of it. Feeds bit production, so it is NOT ignored when `cost_mode ==
  direct` (unlike `*_capex_per_wafer` above).

**Why `combo_capex_per_wafer` / `combo_gross_die` are shared but
`combo_qlc_*` / `combo_tlc_*` are recipe-specific**: the combo line is one
physical line running one physical die design, so investment per wafer and
dies-per-wafer don't change when you switch recipes — only how many bits
each cell stores (density) and how well it yields at that density (and how
fast it ramps to that yield) differ by mode. The dedicated scenario has no
such sharing: `qlc_*` and `tlc_*` are two completely separate die designs
and lines.

**Why there are four independent max-capa fields (`qlc_max_capa` /
`tlc_max_capa` / `combo_qlc_max_capa` / `combo_tlc_max_capa`) instead of one
shared pool**: dedicated is still a sweep over the same 0-100% QLC:TLC ratio
as combo, but each line's 100%-utilization ceiling is its own investment,
not a slice of a fab-wide total. At ratio `r`, the QLC-only line runs at
`r%` of ITS OWN `qlc_max_capa` and the TLC-only line runs at `(100-r)%` of
ITS OWN `tlc_max_capa` — the two dedicated lines can have different max
capacities because they're different lines/investments. Combo is
physically one line that time-shares `r%` QLC-recipe / `(100-r)%`
TLC-recipe and is always 100% utilized, but its per-recipe max capa
(`combo_qlc_max_capa` / `combo_tlc_max_capa`) is still entered separately
since the two recipes can yield slightly different maximum throughput on
the same physical line. A single shared pool would have forced all four
lines/recipes to assume the same total investment size, which is not a
valid assumption across genuinely separate lines.

**Bit production and cost formulas** (see "What this skill does" above):
`bit_per_wafer = density_per_die * grossdie_per_wafer * (mature_yield_pct/100)`
for the monthly snapshot; `cost(line) = (capex_per_10k_wafer/10000) *
wafer_count`, plus each scenario's one-time `dev_cost` from headcount + mask
count ONLY (model mode) or the two direct cent/GB inputs (direct mode). State
these formulas, the unit conventions (mature yield %, ramp coefficient, capex
per 10K wafer, Gb→GB ÷8, ER-wafer rate wafer/월), the dev-cost numbers, each
line's/recipe's own t95, and the ER-wafer-rate assumption
(`calc.ER_WAFER_RATE_ASSUMPTION`) in `report.md` so a domain reviewer can
sanity-check them against their own numbers.

## Dual-mode invocation contract

### Mode A — CLI batch (`frame run` sets env vars)

The CLI exports `$FRAMEAI_RUN_MODE=cli`, `$FRAMEAI_RUN_INPUTS`,
`$FRAMEAI_RUN_OUTPUTS`, `$FRAMEAI_RUN_TEXT`. Bind:
- `inputs_dir = $FRAMEAI_RUN_INPUTS`
- `outputs_dir = $FRAMEAI_RUN_OUTPUTS`
- `text_arg = $FRAMEAI_RUN_TEXT`

### Mode B — Chat invocation (`/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator ...`)

`$FRAMEAI_RUN_MODE` is empty or unset. Self-bootstrap:

1. `ts = <current UTC ISO timestamp, slashes→hyphens>` (e.g. via `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%SZ`)
2. `runs_dir = automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/runs/<ts>/`
3. `mkdir -p "$runs_dir/inputs" "$runs_dir/outputs"`
4. If the user dropped a params file into chat or referenced a path, copy it
   into `runs_dir/inputs/` (Read+Write, or `cp` if it's already in the repo).
   If the user instead pasted `key=value` lines directly in chat, Write them
   to `runs_dir/inputs/params.txt`.
5. Bind `inputs_dir = $runs_dir/inputs`, `outputs_dir = $runs_dir/outputs`,
   `text_arg = $ARGUMENTS`.

From here the algorithm is mode-agnostic — only touches `inputs_dir`,
`outputs_dir`, `text_arg`. Never write outside `outputs_dir`.

## Algorithm

### Phase 1 — Locate params

Look for a `.txt` file under `inputs_dir`. If more than one exists, prefer
one named `params.txt`, else the first alphabetically.

If none found, check whether `text_arg` itself contains `key=value` lines
(at least one line matching `word=value`) — if so, Write it to
`inputs_dir/params.txt` and use that.

If `text_arg` explicitly asks for a genuinely empty calculator (contains
words like "blank", "빈 화면", "empty", "비어있는", "빈 계산기"), generate the
blank scaffold and skip straight to Phase 5:

```bash
python3 skills/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/helpers/render_html.py --blank "$outputs_dir/visualization.html"
```

This writes only `visualization.html` (no `sweep.csv`/`sweep.json`/
`report.md` — there is nothing computed yet to report, per this project's
no-fabricated-numbers rule). Every one of the 30 fab-parameter fields opens
blank; the page shows an inline error banner until they're all filled in.

Otherwise, if still nothing usable (no params file, no `key=value` text, no
explicit blank request), **do not open an empty page** — use the bundled
sample instead so the user sees a working example immediately:

```bash
params_path="automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/samples/sample-1.txt"
```

and continue through Phases 2-4 normally with `--sample` passed to `calc.py`
(see Phase 2) so the output is clearly flagged as sample data throughout.

- **Mode B (chat)**: tell the user no params were found, so the calculator
  was pre-filled with sample data (point at `visualization.html`'s on-page
  banner) — they can type real fab parameters directly into the browser to
  replace the sample values (values never leave the page), press "전체
  비우기" for a genuinely blank start, or paste `key=value` params in chat
  to pre-fill for real next time. Point at
  `automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/samples/sample-1.txt` as an
  optional template for the params-file route.
- **Mode A (CLI, headless)**: report that the run used the bundled sample
  (name the file) and exactly which keys are required for a real run
  (referencing the sample file path), so a human can supply a params file
  or fill in `visualization.html` by hand next time. Do not guess real
  values.

### Phase 2 — Compute (deterministic)

```bash
python3 skills/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/helpers/calc.py "$params_path" "$outputs_dir" --step 5
```

Add `--sample` to this call whenever `params_path` is the bundled sample
file from the Phase 1 fallback (never add it for a real params file or
chat-pasted `key=value` text) — it marks `sweep.json`/`summary.json` with
`is_sample: true` so `visualization.html` and `report.md` both flag the
output as sample/demo data.

This writes `sweep.csv`, `sweep.json`, `summary.json` into `outputs_dir` and
prints a JSON digest (`crossover`, `dev_cost`, `full_buildout`, `cost_mode`,
`sweep_rows`) to stdout. Parse that digest.

If it exits non-zero: read stderr (a `ParamsError` message naming the
missing/invalid key) and report it verbatim to the user — do not retry with
invented values.

### Phase 3 — Render visualization

```bash
python3 skills/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/helpers/render_html.py "$outputs_dir/sweep.json" "$outputs_dir/visualization.html"
```

### Phase 4 — Write report.md

Using `summary.json` / `sweep.json`, write `outputs_dir/report.md` with:
- If `is_sample` is true, an opening line stating plainly that this run used
  bundled sample data, not real fab numbers.
- Combo scenario: bit production and cost/bit at the ratio that maximizes
  bit production (`best_combo_bit_production`), and at the ratio that
  minimizes cost/bit (`best_combo_cost_per_bit`) — report BOTH the raw
  figure and its GB-converted counterpart (`*_bit_total_gb` in 억GB,
  `*_cost_per_gb` in `{currency_unit}/GB`, both already present on the row
  object, no extra math needed). If `cost_mode == "direct"`, state plainly
  that these cost/cost-per-bit figures are not applicable in this run and
  report `direct_cost_per_gb.combo` / `direct_cost_per_gb.dedicated`
  (cent/GB) instead.
- Dedicated scenario: the same two figures
  (`best_dedicated_bit_production` / `best_dedicated_cost_per_bit`), also
  with their GB-converted counterparts — now a full sweep result, not a
  fixed baseline. Same direct-mode caveat as above.
- Crossover, framed as the TLC demand trade-off: if `crossover.exists`,
  state the QLC:TLC ratio at which combo starts beating dedicated on
  cost/bit (based on the monthly, mature-yield snapshot) — i.e. below that
  ratio (more TLC demand), dedicated's true TLC recipe usually wins; above
  it (less TLC demand), combo's cheaper shared capex usually wins. If it
  doesn't exist, state plainly that one strategy wins across the whole
  0-100% range in this parameter set. In `direct` cost mode, note that the
  crossover is still computed from the model-based cost fields in
  `sweep.json` (always present) even though the display layer treats them
  as not-applicable — mention this discrepancy explicitly rather than
  silently reporting a crossover the user can't reconcile with the direct
  inputs.
- One-time dev costs (model mode only — state "해당 없음" in direct mode):
  `dev_cost.combo` and `dev_cost.dedicated` (added once, flat, to every
  ratio's total cost) — each is the sum of a headcount component
  (`*_headcount * coef_cost_per_headcount`) and a mask component
  (`*_mask_count * coef_cost_per_mask`) ONLY; break out both so a reviewer
  can see where the number comes from. State plainly that ER wafers no
  longer contribute a separate one-time cost term — `wafer_total *
  capex_rate_per_wafer` (the regular per-wafer cost) already pays for every
  wafer processed, ER or not.
- ER period / sale-vs-total bit split: state each line's/recipe's own t95
  (`t95_months.qlc` / `.tlc` / `.combo_qlc` / `.combo_tlc`, months to 95% of
  its own mature yield) and the monthly ER-wafer rate applied to it
  (`er_wafer_combo` / `er_wafer_dedicated`), and restate
  `er_wafer_rate_assumption` verbatim (states the rate is applied in full,
  NOT split 50/50, to both lines/recipes of a scenario) so a reviewer can
  judge whether that fits their fab.
- 1-year AND 5-year cumulative view: at the same best-ratio rows used above,
  report each scenario's `one_year_*_bit_total_gb` and `five_year_*_bit_total_gb`
  (억GB, both computed via the actual month-by-month yield-ramp walk, not a
  flat multiplication — say so; production starts at month 1 of both
  windows, no development-months skip), each window's corresponding
  `*_sale_bit_total_gb` (bit actually available to sell, net of the ER
  period's wafer consumption), and `five_year_*_cost_per_gb` /
  `one_year_*_cost_per_gb`.
- Yield ramp-up parameters: state each line's/recipe's `*_mature_yield` and
  `*_yield_ramp_coef`, and that the monthly snapshot/crossover assume full
  ramp (mature yield) while the 1-year/5-year cumulative bit totals are the
  only figures that reflect the ramp-up period.
- Full max-capa buildout (model mode only — state "해당 없음" in direct
  mode): `full_buildout.combo` and `full_buildout.dedicated`
  (ratio-independent totals), and restate `full_buildout.combo_assumption`
  (`calc.COMBO_FULL_CAPEX_ASSUMPTION`) verbatim so a reviewer can judge
  whether `combo_qlc_max_capa` is the right representative capacity for
  their fab.
- One-paragraph recommendation in plain language.
- The bit/cost formulas from the Input format section above (including the
  mature-yield %, ramp coefficient, capex/10K-wafer, and Gb→GB ÷8 unit
  conventions), restated briefly so a reviewer can sanity-check them
  against their own numbers.
- Language matches the input params file / text_arg (Korean → Korean,
  English → English).

### Phase 5 — Report

Print, one per line, the relative paths of every output written. Normal run
(params were found/computed, including the sample fallback):

```
Outputs:
- automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/runs/<ts>/outputs/sweep.csv
- automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/runs/<ts>/outputs/sweep.json
- automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/runs/<ts>/outputs/visualization.html
- automations/qlc-tlc-bit-cost-calculator/runs/<ts>/outputs/report.md
```

If the run used the sample fallback, add one line noting that and how to
get a real run (supply a params file) or a truly blank one (ask for
"blank", press "전체 비우기" in the page, or `render_html.py --blank`).

Blank-scaffold run (explicit blank request in Phase 1): only
`visualization.html` exists — print that single path and remind the user
it opens with every fab-parameter field blank, ready to type into.

In CLI mode the same paths sit under `$FRAMEAI_RUN_OUTPUTS`.

## Error handling

- No params file, no parseable `text_arg`, and no explicit blank request →
  fall back to the bundled sample (`--sample` on `calc.py`) per Phase 1.
  Never invent parameter values for a REAL run — the sample fallback is
  clearly labeled sample data throughout, so it never gets mistaken for a
  computed result.
- Explicit blank request → generate the blank-scaffold `visualization.html`
  per Phase 1 instead of the sample fallback.
- `calc.py` non-zero exit → surface the exact `ParamsError` message; do not
  retry with guessed values. `visualization.html` is still usable
  client-side even when `calc.py` fails on a malformed params file — offer
  the blank scaffold (`render_html.py --blank`) as a fallback so the user
  isn't blocked.
- `render_html.py` failure (either mode) → retry once. If it fails again and
  a normal run was in progress, still deliver `sweep.csv` / `sweep.json` /
  `report.md` and tell the user the HTML visualization is unavailable this
  run. If it was a blank-scaffold run, report the failure — there is nothing
  else to deliver.
- Filesystem write failure inside `outputs_dir` → stop with the OS error; do
  not retry against a different path.
- Empty/invalid values typed directly into `visualization.html` (blank field,
  non-numeric text) are handled client-side: the required field is outlined
  and an error banner lists which fields need attention; the sweep/charts do
  not compute (and do not silently show stale or fabricated numbers) until
  every required field is valid. Fields disabled by the `cost_mode ==
  direct` toggle are exempt from this validation (they're greyed out and
  ignored, not required).

## Limits / honest framing

- **`visualization.html` values never leave the browser**: typing fab
  parameters directly into the page does not write them back to any params
  file or archive — closing the tab discards them. If the user wants the
  numbers preserved across sessions, tell them to save a params file (or
  copy the values out) themselves; this skill does not do it automatically.
- **Default-sample fallback is clearly labeled, but still synthetic**: when
  no params are supplied, the page pre-fills with `samples/sample-1.txt`
  and shows an on-page banner + `report.md` opening line saying so — but a
  user who skims past the banner could still mistake the numbers for a real
  fab result. Always point out the banner explicitly when reporting a
  sample-fallback run (see Phase 5).
- **Capex-per-wafer model only (in `model` cost mode)**: `cost(line) =
  (capex_per_10k_wafer/10000) * wafer_count`, plus a one-time dev cost
  (headcount + mask ONLY — see below for why ER wafers aren't a separate
  cost term). No per-wafer operating cost (materials, labor, utilities)
  beyond capex is modeled — if your decision needs opex, add it to the
  params and adjust `helpers/calc.py` (and the mirrored JS in
  `render_html.py`) before trusting the output.
- **`direct` cost mode is a flat, ratio-independent input, not a
  re-derivation**: `combo_direct_cost_per_gb` / `dedicated_direct_cost_per_gb`
  are taken as-is for every point of the sweep — this mode cannot represent
  a cost/GB that genuinely varies by QLC:TLC ratio (e.g. because of
  economies of scale at one end). If that's needed, stay in `model` mode
  and adjust the underlying capex/dev-cost parameters instead.
- **Yield ramp-up is a single shared functional form
  (Normalized Exponential Saturation) for all four lines/recipes**: real
  ramp curves can be S-shaped, have plateaus, or follow a different
  saturation law entirely. If a line's/recipe's actual ramp behavior
  doesn't fit `mature_yield * (1 - exp(-ramp_coef * t))`, this model can't
  represent that without editing `calc.py`'s `yield_ramp_fraction()` (and
  its JS mirror in `render_html.py`).
- **1-year/5-year cumulative bit totals assume production starts at month 1
  of both windows** (no development-months skip at all anymore — each
  line's/recipe's own t95 marks when its ER period ends, not when
  production starts), and assume the wafer volume at a given ratio stays
  constant across the whole window (only the yield ramps, not the wafer
  count) — a real ramp-up period sometimes also ramps physical wafer starts
  gradually, which this model does not represent.
- **ER-wafer rate is applied in full, independently, to both lines/recipes
  of a scenario — not split**: `er_wafer_combo` / `er_wafer_dedicated` are
  each a single monthly rate consumed up to in full by BOTH of a scenario's
  two lines/recipes during each one's own ER period
  (`calc.ER_WAFER_RATE_ASSUMPTION`), mirroring how `combo_headcount` /
  `dedicated_headcount` are also single scenario-level values used as-is.
  If your fab's ER wafers actually split unevenly (or the two lines/recipes
  draw from a genuinely shared, capacity-limited ER budget instead of two
  independent rates), this model can't represent that without editing
  `calc.py`'s `sweep()`/`window_totals()`.
- **ER wafers no longer carry a separate one-time cost term**: the old "ER
  wafer count × capex_per_wafer" line item was removed outright (not
  zeroed) because `wafer_total * capex_rate_per_wafer` — unchanged —
  already pays for every wafer processed, ER or not. If your fab's ER
  wafers genuinely cost MORE per wafer than a normal production wafer
  (e.g. extra characterization steps), this model can't represent that
  without editing `calc.py`'s cost formulas.
- **Headcount/mask cost coefficients are flat and shared**:
  `coef_cost_per_headcount` / `coef_cost_per_mask` are single rates applied
  identically whether the headcount/mask count belongs to the combo or
  dedicated scenario — only the COUNTS differ per scenario, not the rate. If
  a real fab's per-head or per-mask cost genuinely differs between combo and
  dedicated development, this model can't represent that without editing
  `calc.py`'s `combo_dev_cost` / `dedicated_dev_cost`.
- **GB is a display-layer conversion, not a re-derivation**: `bit_total_gb` /
  `cost_per_gb` fields are the raw Gb sweep numbers divided by 8 — they do
  not re-run the physical calculation in GB units. If your fab's actual
  Gb→GB relationship differs from the standard 1 GB = 8 Gb (e.g. due to ECC
  overhead or a different bit-packing convention), edit the `GB_PER_GIGABIT`
  constant in `calc.py` (and its JS mirror in `render_html.py`).
- **Dev cost is flat, not volume-scaled**: `dev_cost.combo` /
  `dev_cost.dedicated` are added once to every ratio's total cost,
  representing a fixed one-time NRE/qualification cost — they do NOT scale
  with how much volume ends up running at that ratio.
- **100% utilization assumption**: both the combo line and the dedicated
  lines are assumed to run with no idle time; each line's OWN max capa
  (`qlc_max_capa` / `tlc_max_capa` / `combo_qlc_max_capa` /
  `combo_tlc_max_capa`) is split linearly by the ratio. Real changeover/setup
  time between recipes (combo) or between building two separate lines
  (dedicated) is not modeled.
- **Combo's shared capex/gross_die assumption**: `combo_capex_per_wafer`
  and `combo_gross_die` are treated as identical between QLC-mode and
  TLC-mode operation of the same physical line. If your combo line's
  investment or die layout genuinely differs by recipe, this model can't
  represent that — fall back to two `combo_capex_per_wafer`-only scenarios
  and compare manually.
- **Full-buildout figure's combo representative capa is a modeling choice,
  not a supplied number**: `combo_qlc_max_capa` is used as combo's buildout
  size (`calc.COMBO_FULL_CAPEX_ASSUMPTION`) because the combo line is built
  around the QLC die design — `combo_tlc_max_capa` or the larger of the two
  would also be defensible for a different fab. If that assumption doesn't
  fit, edit `calc.py`'s `full_buildout()` (and its JS mirror in
  `render_html.py`).
- **t95 (ER period length) depends only on `*_yield_ramp_coef`, not
  `*_mature_yield`**: this is a direct consequence of the 95%-of-mature
  definition (the mature yield cancels out of the equation), not a
  simplifying choice — a line/recipe with a lower mature yield does not get
  a shorter or longer ER period purely because of that lower yield.
- **"기준 시점" toggle is a pure display lens, not persisted anywhere**:
  switching the first two charts/table to "특정 개월 기준" never touches
  `sweep.csv`/`sweep.json`/`report.md` or the crossover judgement — those
  stay on the mature-yield base sweep always. Reloading the page resets the
  toggle back to "성숙 수율 기준".
- **Not a process simulator**: this is a linear cost/output comparison
  calculator, not a fab-scheduling or process-integration model. Use it for
  first-pass strategic comparison, not final investment sign-off.
